description: White has 6.5 points komi, japanese rules (important!), no prisoners.
sgf:
(;FF[4]GM[1]CA[UTF-8]AP[goproblems:0.1.0]SZ[9]ST[0]AB[ab][bc][ba][ca][cb][ea][eb][fb][gb][ga][gc][hd][he][ge][hh][fh][eh][fg][ff][gg]AW[hg][hf][gf][gh][dh][dg][eg][ef][ee][fe][fd][gd][fc][ec][dc][db][da][cc][bd][ae][dd][de](;B[ac];W[hi](;B[gi];W[ih](;B[ii];W[hc](;B[ig];W[hb];B[ha]C[RIGHT])(;B[if];W[hb];B[ha]C[RIGHT]))(;B[gh];W[ei];B[ii];W[ad]C[Black loses by half a point.]))(;B[ih];W[gi]))(;B[gi];W[ac];B[ad];W[hc]C[You have to respond to this ko threat. After that, white can ignore any ko threat you make and win by finishing the ko.

Note: white could win by an even larger margin by responding to your ko threats , e.g. b D1, since white also has more threats. In any case, black will lose the ko and thus the game.])(;B[di];W[ac];B[ad];W[ei]C[You have to respond to this ko threat. After that, white can ignore any ko threat you make and win by finishing the ko.

Note: white could win by an even larger margin by responding to your ko threats , e.g. b D1, since white also has more threats. In any case, black will lose the ko and thus the game.](;B[fi];W[hi];B[gi];W[ih];B[ii];W[ac]C[You can only win one of the kos, which is not enough to win the game.])(;B[gi];W[ac];B[ci];W[ad]C[Black's reduction on the bottom left is not enough to win.

 (White's best move would be C2, since white has more ko threats, which would win by a larger margin. This is just to illustrate a clear ending to the variation.)]))(;B[ig];W[ac];B[ad];W[ei]C[You have to respond to this ko threat. source: Bottom right corner is from O Meien’s book "Absolute Endgame", Chapter 3, Problem F.
